title: Mixed Graph: The charts below give information about the high-speed continuous internet conne...
description: The charts below give information about the high-speed continuous internet connection of households in five countries in 2001 and 2002. Summ
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Table \+ Bar chart: Internet connections (2001–2002)

1. Giải thích đề

Bảng: phần trăm tăng trưởng kết nối Internet theo quốc gia.

Biểu đồ cột: số lượng kết nối trên mỗi 1000 người (2001 vs 2002).

→ Dạng: mixed (comparison \+ change)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

percentage change

internet connections per 1000 people

Mẫu:

The table shows the percentage change in internet connections across five countries, while the bar chart compares the number of connections per 1,000 people in 2001 and 2002.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Tất cả các nước đều tăng

USA và South Korea có số lượng kết nối cao nhất

Các nước châu Âu thấp hơn nhiều

Britain có mức tăng lớn nhất (+230%), South Korea thấp nhất (+20%)

4. BODY 1 – Bar chart (actual numbers)

USA:

2001: 93 → 2002: 251.1 (tăng mạnh, cao nhất)

South Korea:

2001: 122 → 2002: 134.4 (cao nhưng tăng nhẹ)

Germany:

2001: 3.4 → 2002: 5.6

Switzerland:

2001: 3.6 → 2002: 7.92

Britain:

2001: 3.3 → 2002: 10.89 (tăng rõ)

→ Ý chính:

  • USA vượt trội về số lượng
  • Europe vẫn ở mức thấp dù có tăng

5. BODY 2 – Table (percentage change)

Britain: \+230% (cao nhất)

USA: \+170%

Switzerland: \+120%

Germany: \+80%

South Korea: \+20% (thấp nhất)

→ Ý chính:

  • Tăng trưởng không đồng đều
  • Nước có base thấp (Britain) tăng mạnh nhất

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

increase significantly

record the highest growth

remain relatively low

a sharp rise

a modest increase

in contrast

The table and bar chart compare the percentage increase and the number of mobile phone connections per 1,000 people in five countries between 2001 and 2002.

All countries experienced growth, although the scale differed considerably. The USA and South Korea recorded much higher connection levels than the other nations, while Britain showed the most significant relative increase.

In terms of percentage change, Britain rose most sharply, with an increase of 230%. The USA also saw substantial growth at 170%, followed by Switzerland at 120%. Germany experienced a more moderate rise of 80%, whereas South Korea had the smallest increase, at just 20%.

Looking at the actual figures, the USA had the highest number of connections, climbing from 93 per 1,000 people in 2001 to about 251 in 2002. South Korea also maintained a high level, increasing from 122 to roughly 134. By contrast, the remaining countries had relatively low figures. Switzerland grew from approximately 3.6 to nearly 8, while Germany rose from about 3.4 to 5.6. Britain, despite its rapid growth, only increased from around 3.3 to just under 11 connections per 1,000 people.
